fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Oklahoma Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Oklahoma - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity oklahoma, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to oklahoma, speech-language pathologist compact oklahoma, oklahoma speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
board.parent_dept: Oklahoma Board of Examiners for SLP and Audiology
board.mailing_address: 3700 N. Classen Blvd., Suite 258, Oklahoma City, OK 73118
board.physical_address: 3700 N. Classen Blvd., Suite 248, Oklahoma City, OK 73118

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: Full credential for independent SLP practice in Oklahoma.

license_types.title: Provisional SLP License
license_types.description: For SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.

steps.title: Create eLicense Account and Start Application
steps.description: Register for an account on the OBESPA eLicense online system and select the appropriate initial license application for Speech-Language Pathologist (either Provisional or Full License).

steps.title: Submit Required Documents
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts verifying a Master's or Doctoral degree in Speech-Language Pathology and official Praxis examination scores to be sent directly to OBESPA. Also, complete and upload any required forms, such as the Supervised Clinical Professional Experience (CPE) plan for provisional applicants.

steps.title: Complete Oklahoma Jurisprudence Exam
steps.description: Complete the Oklahoma Jurisprudence Examination module, which is typically integrated into the eLicense online application process.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Remit the non-refundable application fee of $100 through the eLicense online application system.

steps.title: Complete Supervised Clinical Professional Experience (if applicable)
steps.description: If applying for a Full License directly or converting from a Provisional License, ensure completion of the required 1260 hours of supervised clinical professional experience and have the supervisor submit the final evaluation directly to OBESPA.

steps.title: Board Review and License Issuance
steps.description: Allow 4 to 6 weeks for processing of a Provisional License and 2 to 4 weeks for a Full License once all required documents, fees, and examination results have been received and reviewed by the Board.

telepractice_text: Oklahoma permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.
provisional_text: Oklahoma issues a Provisional SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.
reciprocity_text: Oklahoma is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.

faqs.question: How much does an Oklahoma SLP license cost?
Is Oklahoma part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
Does Oklahoma allow telepractice for SLPs?
Does Oklahoma have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Oklahoma participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Oklahoma without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Oklahoma?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Oklahoma while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Oklahoma is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.

hero.title: Become a Speech-Language Pathologist in Oklahoma
hero.dek: To apply for a Speech-Language Pathologist license in Oklahoma, applicants must register and complete the application through the Oklahoma Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology's (OBESPA) eLicense online application system.

official_links.registration_steps: Applicants must create an account on the OBESPA eLicense online system by selecting 'Register' to begin the application process.
